rationale: Authority data for W40K-0101..W40K-0110 — the 2019-2020 cluster spanning the 9th-edition lead-up: Adepta Sororitas as primary protagonist faction (twice: *Mark of Faith* + *Ephrael Stern: The Heretic Saint*, plus secondary in *Requiem Infernal*), Necrons as primary protagonist faction (twice: *The Infinite and the Divine* + co-primary in *Indomitus*), Orks as primary protagonist faction (*Brutal Kunnin*, first authority-layer Ork-POV), Navis Nobilite as primary protagonist faction (*Rites of Passage*, first authority-layer Navigator-POV novel), Carcharodons / Space Sharks as primary protagonist faction (*Silent Hunters*, first authority-layer Carcharodons entry). Bookends: Rachel Harrison's *Honourbound* (2019, Severina Raine commissar-POV opener — Eleventh Antari Rifles in the Bale Stars Crusade vs the Sighted Chaos cult, three-arc structure with present campaign + Schola-Progenium/sister-Lucia flashbacks + intra-Crusade malfeasance subplot) and Edoardo Albert's *Silent Hunters* (2021, Carcharodons brothers-separated-by-feud novel — Pale Nomad Te Kahurangi + Chaplain Tangata Manu pursuing a stolen relic into Commorragh / the Dark City; Chapter-serf POVs Iraia + Jona). Heart of the batch: Peter Fehervari's *Requiem Infernal* (2019, Sister-Hospitaller Asenath Hyades returns to her Last-Candle birthworld Candleworld escorting traumatised Astra-Militarum survivors of a xenos encounter — Fehervari Dark-Coil register, malign forces stirring in storm-wracked spires + insidious shadows in the seekers' souls; Jonah Tythe parallel-pilgrimage POV), Guy Haley's *Belisarius Cawl: The Great Work* (2019, Cawl + Tetrarch Felix + Scythes-of-the-Emperor remnants on the ruined homeworld Sotha — Necron tomb-raid under the chapter monastery + Cawl reckoning with 10,000 years of lost/deleted/stolen memories, ancient evil + residual Tyranid infestation), Mike Brooks's *Rites of Passage* (2019, Lady Chettamandey Brobantis Navigator-POV — frail-old-woman-with-sharp-tongue widow-and-murderer of Lord Azariel vs Chaos cultist Radimir Niklau harvesting Navigator warp-eyes after his Galimo-Prime ritual on Vorlese; first authority-layer entry with House Brobantis + Navis Nobilite as primary protagonist), Rachel Harrison's *Mark of Faith* (2020, Sister Evangeline of Order of Our Martyred Lady + Inquisitor Ahri Ravara crossing the Great Rift into Imperium Nihilus to recover the Shield of Saint Katherine — Ravara's Thousand-Sons-Sorcerer subplot with the crippled-interrogator-Sofika in a miniature Golden Throne), David Annandale's *Ephrael Stern: The Heretic Saint* (2020, Stern the Thrice-Born / Daemonifuge / Heretic Saint + Harlequin Kyganil 100-year webway pilgrimage to gather holy relics and reach the Black Library — webway encounter with Yvraine, Herald of Ynnead, frames the Imperium-Nihilus / Guilliman-returns context), Mike Brooks's *Brutal Kunnin* (2020, Goff Big-Mek Ufthak Blackhawk vs Adeptus-Mechanicus forge world Hephaesto + Freebooterz Kaptin Badrukk + grot Nizkwik + Mogrot Redtoof — first authority-layer entry where Orks are the primary protagonist faction, dropping grimdark for `satirical + action_heavy` register), Robert Rath's *The Infinite and the Divine* (2020, dual-Necron rivalry between Trazyn the Infinite and Orikan the Diviner — Orikan needs a device from Trazyn's Solemnace galleries to find the last true Necrontyr Phaeron's tomb on Cepharil/Serenade; Triarch Council forces them into co-op against a Genestealer-Cult uprising and the resulting Imperial exterminatus; spans millennia, ends with Trazyn-escape + Orikan-stranded), and Gav Thorpe's *Indomitus* (2020, 9th-edition launch novel — Crusade Fleet Quintus's *Ithraca's Vengeance* under Captain Aeschelus + Lts Nemetus and Praxamedes stranded in a becalmed-warp system inside the Pariah Nexus; dual-POV with Necron Overlord Simut + Plasmancer Ah-hotep + Skorpekh-Lord Zozar; the choice between last-stand against the Silent Kingdom and break-away-to-warn-Guilliman is the structural pivot). Structural notes: a heavy female-POV batch — Severina Raine + Asenath Hyades + Lady Chettamandey + Evangeline / Ravara + Ephrael Stern — **five of ten books have female primary POVs**, the strongest such ratio in the authority layer to date. Dense new first-authority-layer surface-forms: Eleventh Antari Rifles + the Sighted Cult + Bale Stars + Order of the Last Candle/Eternal Candle + Candleworld + House Brobantis + Vorlese + Galimo Prime + Lord Azariel + Lady Chettamandey Brobantis + Radimir Niklau + Sotha + Scythes of the Emperor + Tetrarch Felix + Order of Our Martyred Lady + Ophelia VII + Shield of Saint Katherine + Inquisitor Ahri Ravara + Sister Evangeline + Sister Augusta + Sofika + Ephrael Stern (Thrice-Born / Daemonifuge / Heretic Saint) + Kyganil + Yvraine + Ynnead / Ynnari + Hephaesto + Ufthak Blackhawk + Mogrot Redtoof + Nizkwik + Kaptin Badrukk + Da Biggest Big Mek + Cepharil / Serenade + Solemnace + Trazyn the Infinite + Orikan the Diviner + Triarch Council + Crusade Fleet Quintus + Ithraca's Vengeance + Captain Aeschelus + Lt Nemetus + Lt Praxamedes + Overlord Simut + Plasmancer Ah-hotep + Skorpekh-Lord Zozar + Pariah Nexus + Silent Kingdom + Carcharodons / Space Sharks + Te Kahurangi (Pale Nomad) + Chaplain Tangata Manu + Iraia + Jona + Sagaraya + Commorragh / Dark City. No `data_conflict` flags this iteration — all ten roster entries are correctly typed as `novel` per Black-Library / Lexicanum / Track-of-Words. No `low_confidence` flags either — all ten entries have strong post-2019 Black-Library coverage. **Resolver-Pre-Note:** cumulativeBefore=100 (Brief-061 algorithm's strict reading: 100 % 50 == 0 → resolver-pause due). Maintainer invoked `skip-50-stop` marker in the session-opening prompt, so the pre-check was overridden; the resolver-pause-block is deferred. Status-log entry annotates `skip-50-stop active` explicitly.

overrides.synopsis: Commissar Severina Raine fights beside the Eleventh Antari Rifles in the Bale Stars Crusade, an Astra Militarum campaign against the Sighted, a Chaos cult that sends bound thrall regiments into the field. As Raine leads the Antari through drop strikes, urban war and a cult-citadel assault, memories of the Schola Progenium and her sister Lucia keep tightening around the question of what loyalty costs. Someone inside the crusade is feeding the enemy its next target. A somber war-story and character study about command, betrayal and the price of being the Emperor's voice.

Sister-Hospitaller Asenath Hyades returns to Candleworld, the storm-lashed sanctuary she fled decades ago, escorting shattered Astra Militarum survivors who need healing after a xenos encounter. The Order of the Last Candle wants outsiders kept away; Asenath wants to test rumors of heresy and quiet the ghosts she left behind. Jonah Tythe, an itinerant preacher with too many past lives, walks the same road. As the convent's spires draw near, Fehervari turns the pilgrimage inward: malign forces are waking in Candleworld, but the worst shadows are in the souls seeking refuge there. Cosmic horror, mystery and faith in a Dark Coil key.

Belisarius Cawl brings Tetrarch Felix, Ultramarines and the battered remnants of the Scythes of the Emperor back to Sotha, the chapter homeworld Hive Fleet Kraken left in ruins. Beneath the old monastery lies a Necron tomb-network tied to Cawl's next secret Great Work, and the Archmagos intends to raid it even while Tyranid remnants and ancient machines stir around him. The expedition becomes a tomb-heist, a war-story and a reckoning with ten thousand years of memories Cawl has lost, deleted or stolen from himself. Philosophical Mechanicus adventure with the heresy of genius glowing at its core.

Lord Azariel of House Brobantis is dead, and his widow Chettamandey knows exactly who killed him: she did. The frail, brilliant Navigator expects to inherit his power, until ritual murders begin targeting the Navis Nobilite and harvesting their warp-eyes. On Vorlese, the Chaos-touched Radimir Niklau turns the aftermath of his Galimo Prime rite into a campaign of surgical assassinations, forcing Chettamandey into a house-politics investigation where every ally has a price. A court-intrigue mystery inside the Navigator Houses, with old age, ambition and warp-sight made dangerous.

After the war for Ophelia VII, Sister Evangeline of the Order of Our Martyred Lady is sent across the Great Rift into Imperium Nihilus to recover the Shield of Saint Katherine. Her companion is Inquisitor Ahri Ravara of the Ordo Hereticus, whose hunt for a Thousand Sons sorcerer is bound to Sofika, the crippled interrogator she once loved and failed to save. With Sister Augusta at Evangeline's side, the pilgrimage becomes a double test of faith: one woman seeking a saint's relic, the other dragging a private vengeance through a broken half of the Imperium. A Sororitas journey-story where miracle, doubt and war all cut the same way.

Ephrael Stern, the Thrice-Born and Heretic Saint, has wandered the galaxy for a century with the Harlequin Kyganil, purging daemons and gathering relics by way of the webway. Then the Great Rift leaves her bereft of the Emperor's light, and a stranger offers a new path toward the knowledge that might save the Imperium. Stern and Kyganil enter the webway again, meet Yvraine, and set their course toward the Black Library while Imperium Nihilus and Guilliman's return reshape the age around them. Mythic Sororitas-by-origin adventure, half pilgrimage and half cosmic-horror character study, with faith operating outside Imperial sanction.

Ufthak Blackhawk, a Goff Big-Mek with more cunning than anyone wants to admit, leads an Ork assault on the forge world Hephaesto and finds Kaptin Badrukk's Freebooterz already looting the prize. Ordered into temporary cooperation by Da Biggest Big Mek, Ufthak sees a chance to make his name by crushing the Adeptus Mechanicus, stealing the best toys on the planet and surviving Badrukk's inevitable double-cross. His grot Nizkwik adds panic, loyalty and comic timing to the carnage. A rare Ork-POV war-heist that swaps solemn grimdark for loud, brutal, very kunnin' satire.

Trazyn the Infinite guards the galleries of Solemnace like a museum, a fortress and a private joke. Orikan the Diviner breaks in to steal a device he believes will lead to the tomb of the last true Necrontyr Phaeron and the secret of undoing biotransference. Their feud stretches across millennia, through lawsuits before the Triarch Council, uneasy alliances, and the human world of Cepharil, also called Serenade, where a signal beneath the surface draws in Necrons, a Genestealer Cult and Imperial extermination. A philosophical, action-heavy rivalry novel with antique arrogance, deadpan comedy and genuine cosmic stakes.

Crusade Fleet Quintus is desperate for a victory when the Ultramarines cruiser Ithraca's Vengeance becomes trapped in a becalmed system inside the Pariah Nexus. Captain Aeschelus and Lieutenants Nemetus and Praxamedes face a Necron technology-grid that drains souls for the Silent Kingdom, while Overlord Simut, Plasmancer Ah-hotep and Skorpekh Lord Zozar press their own fractured agenda. What begins as a crusade rescue turns into a choice with no clean honor: die trying to break the Nexus, or abandon the sector so Guilliman can be warned. A 9th-edition on-ramp built as last-stand war-story, Necron mystery and new-era primer.

The Carcharodons return from the void-haunted galactic fringe when a relic Chaplain Tangata Manu has hunted for a thousand years is stolen and carried into Commorragh, the Dark City of the Drukhari. The chase brings Te Kahurangi, the Pale Nomad Librarian, back into the hunt and exposes an old feud between brothers that the mission can no longer leave buried. Chapter-serfs Iraia and Jona give the quest a human pulse as the trail runs from the sea world Sagaraya to the worst city in the galaxy, where every bargain cuts. A grim Carcharodons journey-heist about relic-obsession, brotherhood and reconciliation under knives.
